Subject: Your UK261 Compensation Claim — Booking Reference BA123456
Date: Mon, 15 Apr 2024 09:23:41 +0100
Message-ID: <20240415092341.BA123456@britishairways.com>

Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

Dear Mr Smith,

Thank you for contacting British Airways regarding your recent flight experience.

We are writing in response to your compensation claim under UK Retained Regulation (EC) No 261/2004 for the following flight:

Flight Number:  BA117
Route:          London Heathrow (LHR) to New York John F. Kennedy (JFK)
Date of Travel: 15 March 2024
Booking Reference: BA123456

After reviewing our operational records, we can confirm that flight BA117 on 15 March 2024 arrived at its destination with a delay of 4 hours 30 minutes. As this delay exceeds the 3-hour threshold defined under UK261 legislation, you are entitled to compensation.

Based on the distance of this route (in excess of 3,500km), the applicable compensation is:

    £520.00 per passenger

We are currently processing your claim. You can expect to receive payment to the bank account details you provided within 14 business days. Your claim reference number is BA123456.

If you have any questions regarding your claim, please do not hesitate to contact our Customer Relations team.

Yours sincerely,

Customer Relations
British Airways
PO Box 1126
Uxbridge, UB8 9XS
United Kingdom

This email and any attachments are confidential and may be legally privileged.
